title: Consider adding Jenkins usage example to K8s plugin repo
body:
Hello all,

I'm trying to wrap my head around how I might use podman within a container in a Jenkins pipeline using the [Jenkins kubernetes-plugin](https://github.com/jenkinsci/kubernetes-plugin) to build and push an image. Is there a pre-built libpod image for this use case? Does the podman "abstraction" over buildah change this [comparison overview](https://github.com/GoogleContainerTools/kaniko#comparison-with-other-tools) in regards to CICD? Might it be possible to submit a PR of an [example Jenkinsfile](https://github.com/jenkinsci/kubernetes-plugin/tree/master/examples) using podman and related best practices? I don't want to rely on public cloud providers always having a running docker daemon on host machines.

A bit of background: I've played with both [img](https://blog.jessfraz.com/post/building-container-images-securely-on-kubernetes/) and Kaniko to build images on Jenkins but have run into a number of issues with both tools (caching, volume permissions, root/privilege issues, Jenkins JNLP timeouts, Kaniko doesn't support separate build and push steps, etc) and I am hoping to get podman working instead.

Thank you.

comments.body:
Specifically for building, you should find little to no difference between Podman and Buildah. `podman build` is actually executing the same code as `buildah bud` - the only difference are different default arguments for a few CLI flags. If you're only looking at building, `buildah` and `podman` should be identical, so the comparison overview for buildah should also apply here. The main difference in `podman` is adding the ability to run and manage containers based on those images.

As such, if you're only building, you might have better luck at the Buildah repo - a few of the Buildah maintainers do browse here, but you'll probably find better answers at the main repo.
@rhatdan Is there a community image for podman or buildah similar to `gcr.io/kaniko-project/executor`, `r.j3ss.co/img`, or `docker.io/dind`?
@TomSweeneyRedHat Have you had a chance to look at this?
@TomSweeneyRedHat curious if you have any updates regarding a community image.
@marshallford Any chance you want to take this on?
I'm interested in the community image, as well - would like to see this land!
@TomSweeneyRedHat is this what you were hacking on the other day?

https://quay.io/user/podman
https://quay.io/user/buildah

Would be the suggested images to use to satisfy this need.  @marshallford if I am incorrect, please reopen this PR,
